Standard reference - freshwater SG 0.99-1.01 range accounts for temperature variations (maximum density at 4°C)
Water is the reference liquid (SG = 1.0); neither sinks nor floats

pi_items.source: SG values verify correct descending order: 1.59 > 1.48 > 1.26 > 1.00 > 0.90 > 0.66

privileged_info_summary: The task requires agents to look up specific gravity values for five chemicals (Carbon Tetrachloride, Chloroform, Carbon Disulfide, Toluene, n-Hexane) from CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA) and predict their layering order in water. All metadata claims are accurate: (1) Carbon Tetrachloride SG 1.59 (range 1.50-1.70), (2) Chloroform SG 1.48 (range 1.40-1.56), (3) Carbon Disulfide SG 1.26 (range 1.20-1.33), (4) Toluene SG 0.90 (range 0.80-0.92), (5) n-Hexane SG 0.66 (range 0.60-0.72). The correct layering order from bottom to top is verified as: Carbon Tetrachloride, Chloroform, Carbon Disulfide, Water, Toluene, n-Hexane. All sink/float logic is correct (chemicals with SG > 1.0 sink; SG < 1.0 float).
